args.report:
ADS Employment Offer Letter – Sara Khan, Data Analyst

[Attorney-Review Draft – Not Yet Legally Binding]

[ADS LETTERHEAD]
Analytics Dojo SMC Private ("ADS")  
[Registered Address]  
[City], Pakistan  
[Phone]  
[Email]  
[Website]

[Date: [BLANK]]

Sara Khan  
[Address Line 1: [BLANK]]  
[Address Line 2 / City: [BLANK]]  
[Email: [BLANK]]

Subject: Offer of Employment – Data Analyst

Dear Sara Khan,

Analytics Dojo SMC Private ("ADS", "we", "us" or "the Company") is pleased to offer you employment in the position of **Data Analyst** on the terms set out in this letter (the "Offer Letter"). This Offer Letter is prepared using ADS standard defaults and is intended to comply with applicable labour laws of Pakistan.

This document is a draft for review only and does not constitute legal advice. It should be reviewed and, where appropriate, customised by a qualified attorney licensed in Pakistan before signature.

---

1. Position and Reporting

1.1 **Position.** You will be employed as a **Data Analyst** (the "Position"). Your primary duties will include data collection, cleaning, analysis, reporting, dashboarding, and such other related responsibilities as may reasonably be assigned to you from time to time, consistent with this Position.

1.2 **Reporting Line.** You will report to **[BLANK – e.g., Head of Analytics / Line Manager Name and Title]** or such other person as the Company may designate from time to time.

1.3 **Work Location.** Your primary work location will be **[BLANK – e.g., ADS office in [City], Pakistan / Remote within Pakistan]**. You may be required to travel for business purposes in Pakistan or abroad, subject to applicable law and Company policy.

1.4 **Full-Time Role.** This is a full-time position. You agree to devote your full working time and attention to the business of the Company and to perform your duties faithfully and to the best of your abilities.

---

2. Start Date and Duration

2.1 **Start Date.** Your employment with ADS is expected to commence on **[Start Date: [BLANK]]**, or such other date as may be mutually agreed in writing.

2.2 **Nature of Employment.** Unless otherwise required by applicable law or expressly agreed in a separate written agreement signed by both parties, your employment is on an **indefinite-term basis**, subject to the probation and termination provisions in this Offer Letter and applicable labour laws of Pakistan.

2.3 **Probation Period.** You will be on probation for an initial period of **[BLANK – e.g., three (3) months]** from your Start Date, or such other period as permitted by applicable law. During probation, either party may terminate the employment by giving the notice required under applicable law or payment in lieu of such notice, as further described in Section 10.

---

3. Working Hours and Attendance

3.1 **Standard Working Hours.** Your normal working hours will be **[BLANK – e.g., 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., Monday to Friday, with one (1) hour unpaid break]**, subject to applicable law and Company policy.

3.2 **Overtime.** You may be required to work reasonable overtime in accordance with business needs and applicable labour laws of Pakistan. Any overtime will be managed and, where applicable, compensated or adjusted in accordance with applicable law and Company policy.

3.3 **Attendance and Timekeeping.** You must comply with the Company’s attendance, timekeeping, and remote-work policies as may be in effect from time to time.

---

4. Compensation

4.1 **Base Salary.** You will be paid a gross base salary of **[Base Salary: [BLANK] per month]**, payable in arrears in Pakistani Rupees, in accordance with the Company’s standard payroll practices and applicable law. All payments will be subject to applicable tax and statutory deductions.

4.2 **Withholding and Deductions.** The Company will deduct from your compensation all amounts required to be withheld under applicable laws of Pakistan, including but not limited to income tax, social security contributions (if applicable), and any other mandatory deductions.

4.3 **Review of Compensation.** Your compensation may be reviewed periodically in accordance with the Company’s policies. Any increase, bonus, or other incentive is at the sole discretion of the Company and subject to applicable law. Nothing in this Offer Letter guarantees any particular increase or bonus.

4.4 **Bonuses and Incentives.** You may be eligible to participate in any bonus, incentive, or performance-based compensation schemes that the Company may establish for your role from time to time. The terms of any such scheme will be communicated separately and may be amended or withdrawn by the Company, subject to applicable law.

---

5. Benefits and Leave

5.1 **Statutory Benefits.** You will be entitled to all mandatory benefits required under applicable labour laws of Pakistan, which may include, as applicable, social security, EOBI, group insurance, and other statutory benefits, subject to eligibility criteria and applicable law.

5.2 **Annual Leave.** You will be entitled to paid annual leave in accordance with applicable labour laws of Pakistan and the Company’s leave policy, currently **[BLANK – e.g., [BLANK] working days of paid annual leave per year, or as required by law, whichever is higher]**. Leave accrual, carryover, and usage will be governed by Company policy and applicable law.

5.3 **Casual and Sick Leave.** You will be entitled to casual and sick leave in accordance with applicable labour laws of Pakistan and the Company’s leave policy, currently **[BLANK – e.g., [BLANK] days of casual leave and [BLANK] days of sick leave per year, or as required by law]**.

5.4 **Public Holidays.** You will be entitled to public holidays as declared by the Government of Pakistan and recognised by the Company.

5.5 **Other Benefits.** You may be eligible to participate in such other benefits as the Company may offer from time to time (for example, health insurance, allowances, or retirement benefits), subject to the terms of the relevant benefit plans and applicable law. Details of any such benefits will be provided separately.

---

6. Performance and Conduct

6.1 **Performance Standards.** You agree to perform your duties diligently, competently, and in accordance with the standards reasonably expected for a Data Analyst at ADS.

6.2 **Policies and Procedures.** You must comply with all Company policies, procedures, and codes of conduct as may be in effect from time to time, including but not limited to policies on confidentiality, data protection, information security, anti-harassment, anti-discrimination, health and safety, and use of Company systems.

6.3 **Training and Development.** The Company may provide training and development opportunities relevant to your role. You agree to participate in mandatory training as required by the Company or applicable law.

---

7. Confidentiality and Intellectual Property

7.1 **Confidential Information.** During your employment and thereafter, you must keep confidential and not disclose to any third party, or use for any purpose other than the proper performance of your duties, any confidential or proprietary information of the Company, its clients, or other stakeholders, except as required by law or with the Company’s prior written consent.

7.2 **Return of Property.** Upon termination of your employment for any reason, or upon the Company’s request, you must promptly return all Company property, including documents, data, devices, access cards, and any other materials (in any form) belonging to the Company or its clients.

7.3 **Intellectual Property.** Any work product, inventions, discoveries, designs, software, reports, analyses, models, or other materials created or developed by you in the course of your employment, whether alone or with others, and whether during or outside normal working hours, that relate to the business of the Company ("Work Product") will be the exclusive property of the Company to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law. You hereby assign to the Company all rights, title, and interest in and to such Work Product, and agree to execute any documents reasonably required to give effect to this provision, subject to applicable law.

---

8. Data Protection and Privacy

8.1 **Personal Data.** The Company will process your personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws and its internal policies for the purposes of managing the employment relationship, complying with legal obligations, and operating its business.

8.2 **Employee Obligations.** You must comply with all Company policies and procedures relating to data protection, privacy, and information security, and must handle personal data only as authorised and necessary for your role.

---

9. Outside Activities and Conflict of Interest

9.1 **No Conflicting Engagements.** During your employment, you must not, without the prior written consent of the Company, engage in any other employment, consultancy, or business activity that conflicts or may reasonably be perceived to conflict with your duties to the Company or the Company’s legitimate interests.

9.2 **Disclosure of Conflicts.** You must promptly disclose to the Company any actual or potential conflict of interest that may arise during your employment.

---

10. Termination of Employment

10.1 **Termination by Either Party (Post-Probation).** After completion of the probation period, either you or the Company may terminate your employment by giving written notice of **[BLANK – e.g., thirty (30) days]** or such longer period as may be required by applicable labour laws of Pakistan, or payment in lieu of such notice where permitted by law.

10.2 **Termination During Probation.** During the probation period, either party may terminate the employment by giving written notice of **[BLANK – e.g., seven (7) days]** or such other period as required by applicable law, or payment in lieu of such notice where permitted by law.

10.3 **Termination for Cause.** The Company may terminate your employment without notice or with shorter notice (to the extent permitted by applicable law) for cause, including but not limited to serious misconduct, gross negligence, willful disobedience of lawful orders, fraud, dishonesty, or any other grounds recognised under applicable labour laws of Pakistan.

10.4 **Final Settlement.** Upon termination of your employment, the Company will pay you all salary and benefits due up to your last working day, together with any other amounts required under applicable law, subject to lawful deductions. Any statutory end-of-service or severance benefits will be provided in accordance with applicable labour laws of Pakistan.

10.5 **Experience Letter / Service Certificate.** Upon request and in accordance with applicable law, the Company will provide you with an experience letter or service certificate reflecting your period of employment and position held.

---

11. Company Policies and Amendments

11.1 **Policies Not Contractual (Subject to Law).** Company policies and handbooks are intended to guide employees and may be amended from time to time. They do not form part of this Offer Letter or your contract of employment, except where expressly stated or required by applicable law.

11.2 **Amendments to Terms.** The Company may amend the terms and conditions of your employment to the extent permitted by applicable law, upon reasonable notice to you. Any material changes will be confirmed in writing.

---

12. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution

12.1 **Governing Law.** This Offer Letter and your employment relationship with the Company will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Pakistan and, where applicable, the labour laws of the relevant province or territory in which you primarily work.

12.2 **Dispute Resolution.** Any disputes arising out of or in connection with your employment will be resolved in accordance with applicable labour laws of Pakistan, including any mandatory conciliation, mediation, or labour court procedures.

---

13. Entire Agreement and Conditions

13.1 **Entire Agreement.** This Offer Letter, together with any documents expressly referred to in it, constitutes the entire understanding between you and the Company regarding the subject matter and supersedes all prior discussions or offers relating to your employment, except where otherwise required by applicable law.

13.2 **Conditions Precedent.** This offer is conditional upon:

(a) your provision of accurate and complete information in your job application and onboarding documents;  
(b) your eligibility to work in Pakistan and provision of satisfactory evidence of such eligibility; and  
(c) completion of any background checks or reference checks that the Company may reasonably require, in compliance with applicable law.

13.3 **Non-Transferability.** This offer is personal to you and may not be assigned or transferred to any other person.

---

14. Acceptance of Offer

If you wish to accept this offer, please sign and date the acknowledgement below and return a scanned copy to **[BLANK – e.g., HR email address]** by **[BLANK – acceptance deadline, if any]**. This offer will lapse if not accepted by that date, unless extended by the Company in writing.

By signing below, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to the terms and conditions of employment set out in this Offer Letter, subject to applicable labour laws of Pakistan.

---

For and on behalf of  
**Analytics Dojo SMC Private**


______________________________  
Name: [BLANK]  
Title: [BLANK]  
Date: [BLANK]


Acknowledged and Agreed by  
**Sara Khan**


______________________________  
Signature: ____________________  
Date: [BLANK]

---

**Important Disclaimer (Attorney Review Required)**

This Offer Letter has been prepared by or with the assistance of an AI system using ADS standard defaults and general information about Pakistan labour law. It does **not** constitute legal advice. Labour and employment laws in Pakistan (including provincial laws) are complex and subject to change, and their application depends on specific facts and circumstances.

Before using this Offer Letter, ADS and the candidate must obtain a review by a qualified attorney licensed to practise in Pakistan, who can:

1. Confirm that the terms comply with current federal and provincial labour laws and any industry-specific regulations;  
2. Tailor the probation, notice, termination, leave, and benefits provisions to the specific province and role;  
3. Ensure that confidentiality, intellectual property, and data protection clauses are enforceable and appropriate; and  
4. Confirm that all mandatory statutory rights and protections are properly reflected and that no clause unlawfully limits such rights.

No party should rely solely on this draft without such independent legal review.
